textByHashes.eec7ed2b659e82398a9d6135b57af5b5499107a9: You too, thanks for freeing me.
textByHashes.1346b6690305107b6b12103b35af4631cd9eb847: It's just a shame the mourners confiscated my equipment.
textByHashes.f59bbd1e883cf2b5844e3b786d37e86d4192fbbd: My distillator. I can't test any plague samples without it. They're holding it in the Mourner Headquarters in West Ardougne.
textByHashes.9490213b1c1f4338e244f41a41b9b795a65188ac: I must somehow retrieve that distillator if I am to find a cure for this awful affliction. Do you think you could help me?
textByHashes.0b412e2dce52dbe40b39303008eec5e2c6e04b42: I was hoping you would say that. Unfortunately they discovered the tunnel and filled it in. We need another way over the wall.
textByHashes.204d79c3d2df94f02f0c038e3da34db93fafaac3: My father's friend Jerico is in communication with West Ardougne. He might be able to help us. He lives in a house south east of here, north of the chapel.
textByHashes.c926bec01d1b318564843c2841edc3765487c034: You're back, did you find the distillator?
textByHashes.3016b37a16cb00ba7c70ce79882b58f73de5b3d1: I can't test the samples without the distillator. Please don't give up until you find it.
textByHashes.393b66e79209703951cc891c4995a92083fb7111: Great! Now I can finally run these tests.
textByHashes.88cfe7be0298d07fa84a38256884421105b14ed6: I don't understand... the touch paper hasn't changed colour at all...
textByHashes.b10c5d5d3137e3432bf8008cd104ed6d1ed57524: I'm not sure. My old mentor in Varrock, Guidor, might know though. Could you take these to him and see what he thinks? You'll need to drop by the chemist in Rimmington first to pick up some more touch paper.
textByHashes.32d22159c560bf080d2f1683d5b94b92d53c23d8: Now, last time I went to see Guidor, some one told the guards I was carrying some plague samples. Annoyingly, that means they've started searching everyone going to that part of the city.
textByHashes.7d39b7ee977fb17f253550bc322d1cede8112da0: Ironically, the guards won't be smart enough to recognise the sample itself, so you should be able to get that in just fine. They'll probably confiscate the rest though.
textByHashes.ddfd2000b70952f881ececcdc543d7a4cead12b2: Well I know the chemist has still been managing to get chemicals to Guidor. Maybe he can help you smuggle the things in.
textByHashes.3030ee8080db4beebe62afee2d26094218811adb: What are you doing back here?
textByHashes.05dbef0ee4f87f703215a281655c9d07809403db: I see... I understand what you mean... But let's discuss that later.
textByHashes.54eb4da4cb282e7a9a5c8f19d2e2f8b870a5a0a7: Are you sure? Looks like you have everything to me.
textByHashes.8eabbe24b384ad231993578fbfc32fe4aabccc1e: There you go. That should be everything.
textByHashes.ec256bf1653701d2fa67d1dca6a22ccaa371aebc: Go to Rimmington and get some touch paper from the chemist. Once you've done that, you'll need to take those things to Guidor in Varrock.
textByHashes.458adf7f31ea5e8a511e75b90aa933e43f08f5dc: You'll probably need to smuggle some of the items in to stop them getting confiscated. If you need help moving them discreetly, the chemist might be able to assist you.
textByHashes.fd533848e96efd171a7a94c358369136f4e04a19: You're back! So what did Guidor say?
textByHashes.ef9711cc3704b0761dfa6cf4bb8ea5f92f01594d: Doesn't exist? But I don't understand... why?
textByHashes.9b9b82ea909214719a53475c2a6c83dd49688a80: Well I think there's only one thing for it. The king must be confronted!
textByHashes.8cb8badebf5bc2320b6ac4631309f28666c8f5c0: Yes. King Lathas of East Ardougne. He was the one that had the wall built. He was the one who called the mourners in. He must know what's really going on here.
textByHashes.dc76c68e2bd1d92a34d0a11823105a812e384865: Be quick. You should find him in the castle.
textByHashes.90fa522a7c77f56abdbe9ce98de18ee6a4976dfa: You must go and see King Lathas immediately!
textByHashes.21521f2f3f471b4a9bf035eaba004f3e740aa99d: Not bad, did you speak to the king?
textByHashes.2310f61982d0a5ae89e09b588eea3dba483ad416: I hope so. Even without the plague, I still worry about the people of West Ardougne.
textByHashes.ee65ef00f99dc6534af2276673953a86a099eda2: Not bad, I do wish you could tell me what's going on with the plague though.
textByHashes.a2962379f293b47b9c957f8306d1670cb046a7f2: I hope that's not too far off.
textByHashes.73e4d4e1eae4f20bc01a2f00ff6d41acbfc9d0bb: This doesn't sound good at all. Should we tell someone?
textByHashes.671ae1335e20854b79d9bee69687d847f7c815b6: Well let me know as soon as you have something. And look after yourself out there, I worry about what you've got yourself involved in.
textByHashes.4cea08a263f69d1e7ef8c811def6768c38cc8518: How dare you enter my house, Mourner! Get out!
textByHashes.9355e5998c86619bcdd626d5b7eb5c7427ac9818: ? I didn't recogonise you in all that Mourner gear!
textByHashes.a431bb104c6cc614d35f134ef9bebb0e1ea54e98: I worry about what you've got yourself involved in. You be careful out there.
textByHashes.c06f3fb751d38c9a3f6aeecc50991ab6343cef37: You'd better. Let me know as soon as you work out what's going on.
textByHashes.1e9be2dbda779fd607437d24bc42e84eb353b3f6: ? I didn't recognise you in all that Mourner gear!
textByHashes.6d545e484ef07ede0ccd771dd4af3ba7bbef989b: Well I'm not going anywhere. What's going on?
textByHashes.2cc0ffa47735f72a095ce247ce149c97ba847c3e: ! How's it going? Have you found out any more about the plague?
textByHashes.a0fb16d957460e3df693fe93bef0299d982a0aae: I see, that's quite the reveal. If you need anything from me just ask.
textByHashes.277c5f66e80313622248f87994f600118f4b7cdd: I doubt any poison based solely on apples, rotten or not, would be very effective.
textByHashes.0c666b7312c663821f131f648f187a9abfd0490d: Hmm... That sounds like they were ill from some sort of toxin, I should think it was a mould of some sort that had the effect, not the rotten apple itself. What do you need it for?
textByHashes.6d2fa69960bba42e37449d8e42ba0d4c2215bde5: That's awful, I can't help you with that.
textByHashes.b91ac69e2096ebb1dddf03204cb4dc680c200858: You'd better be right about this, and I'd better make sure you get this toxin right so no one dies! Bring me a sample of rotten apple to examine. I'll also see if I can make a counteractant for the toxin.
textByHashes.ac3a6077366913ba1ab921a54c582d659647fa75: You have a sample of rotten apple for me?
textByHashes.b269180097827b13ea6fb64ddf4188cc71e3475c: Well if you bring me one we can get started on this toxin.
textByHashes.d85f9c3639970c9edf002aa9605886f0a63174fa: Ick... Alright then let's get started.
textByHashes.683e3a4e41c906dda289aa04056a75dc843d2a2b: Okay, I've managed to isolate a small sample of the toxin. It's a byproduct of the mould that grows on these apples, it's not fatal so a counteractant shouldn't be necessary. How big is the store that you're going to affect?
textByHashes.3558b6e6068b86ec3c864c6fd180746085a4f766: That's over half of the food in the city! You're going to need a huge amount of the toxin to do that. You're also going to need to refine it too, or people will notice rotten apples in amongst the food.
textByHashes.3617748f0a4be733e109270cc4a5fde345f7fae0: I would, but I don't have the right equipment here to do anything in bulk.
textByHashes.10e3d2d4c2aec173ae0ed8d58561139e0cecb97d: Right then, the first thing to do is mash up a lot of rotten apples, then you will need to dissolve the toxin into a liquid that has a very low evaporation point, some form of solvent. I don't know as much about those kinds of chemicals but I believe naphtha will be perfect for this.
textByHashes.0dcaefcd73720b878f961f045ea65f6cc8a96b41: Perfect. Well get yourself some naphtha and some mashed rotten apples and mix them together. Once you've done that, strain out any solids. Finally, heat the mixture to evaporate off the solvent. Be careful of naked flames as the solvent will be highly flammable.
textByHashes.6e3561f10abf3a65719cf546eb65b6530a499e76: Sorry, I never said it would be simple. Here, you'll need this to strain out the solids.
textByHashes.b0a38b871c1005a821e8b69c0da39e65d9423f93: You may want to check out the orchard just north of the city. I hear no one has tended it since the blight infected the trees there. I imagine it will have plenty of rotten apples.
textByHashes.cf1a684d9e2c26b6303aa637b6c436b3ad6c5b50: Huh? Oh hello. I just can't get used to a friend being dressed as mourner.
textByHashes.db3d995746a57ddb2302ed38df5f97158acbffe7: Right, the first thing to do is mash up a lot of rotten apples. You'll probably be able to do that at the orchard just north of the city. I hear no one has tended it since the blight infected the trees there. Next, you'll want to get yourself some naphtha and mix it with the rotten apples. You mentioned the chemist in Rimmingon helped you with naphtha before. You might want to speak to him again. Once that's done, use the sieve I gave you to strain out any solids. Finally, heat the mixture to evaporate off the solvent. Be careful of naked flames as the solvent will be highly flammable.
textByHashes.5dba588f106ee9276a3719d818db3d06a4d15512: So have you found out what those mourner are up to yet?
textByHashes.e131c5c593d1913e3ac28825a957ee294efad1a7: Well let me know when you find out.
textByHashes.7de919eec9321920790f3e8f38a09767e857d10d: Hello . Have you found out what those mourners are up to yet?
textByHashes.cbfd198871ef58c18628001f5acb61b85e72111e: Hey get me out of here please!
textByHashes.f983fa80ee4d01d54b09ff390f36658c1d313d2b: I think there may be one around somewhere. I'm sure I heard them stashing it somewhere.
textByHashes.636a34cf912ccd874bd9c584ec68b223e7823987: No, I have none of the symptoms.
textByHashes.d603f13919ac544e6d222c392e7a579690dba4e2: I suppose that was a cover up by the kidnappers.
textByHashes.430c32b356c7e0ea814c2ef10cf92f82171368c6: Thank you, being kidnapped was so inconvenient. I was on my way back to East Ardougne with some samples, I want to see if I can diagnose a cure for this plague.
textByHashes.5969346d48e816b5ffdc509b757d658b03c36d14: Go and see my father, I'll make sure he adequately rewards you. Now I'd better leave while I still can.
textByHashes.191a06fa4b61fbaaca7dce54c13848973fa416c1: Oh thank goodness. It's awful in here. The smell from that mugger...
textByHashes.2bf781482fad5785d8931ff12fef1b0789be4d81: Oh it's quite the tale, but let's save it for until I'm out of here. What's your plan?
textByHashes.4cac864434d194742927cd1f7089e73acda1a332: You don't have a plan? But aren't you meant to be an adventurer? How do you normally do this sort of thing?
textByHashes.5778a72b261ead4ec75188c9d7365da8edc24e2d: Well luckily for us I have something in mind myself. There's an iron grate at the back of this cell that looks like it leads into the sewers. If I had some sulphuric acid, I could melt through it and escape.
textByHashes.b1006ef639a1092e62fe5415af97a59154c981e4: Thankfully it's pretty much essential in my line of work. I have some at my home, opposite my parents' house. You'll need to get it from my cabinet and bring it here. It's the thick clear liquid.
textByHashes.dec0ad7cae81c52b491a0b2ff90538006f17741a: Ah , thank goodness. How's it going?
textByHashes.00038d51bde11d64cf868f29ef9bd6e0f9c273fa: Go to the cabinet in my home, opposite my parents' house. Find some sulphuric acid and bring it back here. It's the thick clear liquid.
textByHashes.b7446ae523c5b78724ca3d95cc2d6f95699be22d: No that's not right at all. You need some sulphuric acid. It's the thick clear liquid.
textByHashes.dc54afa91c813c79c17c4af440726cf0cd02fafe: Yes that's it! Hand it over please.
textByHashes.a10c1f0283a9948df4725938068a9fe69ea9dd26: Right, probably best you're not here when I escape. You should head back to my father.
textByHashes.8a2c790d363fa972f5d26c4ad4e0a8dea8c51c06: Now's not the time for games !
textByHashes.42eb8fdfeffdbdd1d244de9c36e00d9356e316c5: And you . Thank you so much for helping to get me out of there.
textByHashes.e45e20844a2df9fc193edfa1c44482451b4d6e82: I care about you as well, . Thank you for not forgetting about me.
textByHashes.ab7040080be1e93444b8ef3e066666f27236dc5d: Yes! It all started when you told me the truth about the king. I realised that it would take more than just us two to overthrow him, so I started to find allies for us.
textByHashes.fd78b1adb2ae3a71ec0415a08063095e064a5189: Well I knew you were busy infiltrating the mourners so I didn't want to trouble you until we were all set up. We're just about there now though.
textByHashes.26ca39ba4f09c803df92d3eb3326c385ebd39c19: It is. It happened while I was trying to gather more support for us. I was trying to convince some people of the truth behind the plague, but they didn't believe me. They reported me and I was arrested not long after.
textByHashes.0127fe3bc397459f571a7393251dca7dc90364b9: With the mourners? What did you find out?
textByHashes.80bffaabc872d6fa014024b2bcb11d3f3815a57b: That doesn't sound good at all.
textByHashes.ef313c551c3fcf66f56f6cedcd7d5804c4c7c6eb: Ah, that's good to hear. Do you think we're safe now?
textByHashes.e8cc8505c8448402edb3abce85ea73cb5299b8f4: Well we can't do anything about Lord Iorwerth, your elf friends will have to sort him out. But if we get our way, Lathas might not be king for much longer.
textByHashes.ec54054f7a0e554a6bd77fcfcba76f4575bdf3dc: You've met Councillor Halgrive before, right? He's been helping us put a plan together.
textByHashes.66bc2595605ec3805c1673b47fb3ffde55d6a2b8: Before I was arrested, we'd hoped to gather enough support among the people to be able to peacefully remove the king from power. Sadly, we've not been able to convince many of them.
textByHashes.d961589ad4725dbf0c8b370ffaacd7b4336d17dd: Councillor Halgrive has come up with an alternative way to gather support. I can't pretend I totally approve of it, but it seems to be our only option.
textByHashes.447ed8712e01b4a7bb97cae6cbfcaa15cefa91c3: You can probably see why I'm not a fan now. If we're to remove the king, it should mark a new start for Ardougne. But if that's all built on a lie, are we any better than the king himself?
textByHashes.e62f3beaf45c34f3aedb04facdc011009f1939b9: But who are we to decide that?
textByHashes.211e849334d16d966ac3524823adba4afe99f163: You're right, it just makes me feel uncomfortable, all of this.
textByHashes.6897ee2fb7c25a7a90ce42149797a5d1b891b150: You'll want to build support in West Ardougne as well but the knights aren't allowed here. You can use your mourner disguise instead though.
textByHashes.6275e55e9c813e9a27bd6c2f3fd994a843b680dd: I'll keep track of how many people we've recruited, just let me know if you need an update.
textByHashes.314fee23d02572c3e386f56f4974385b66c84ebd: Hey. We haven't recruited anyone into the resistance yet. You should speak to the others if you need help getting started.
textByHashes.7e2c28191afa316be45dda832dea370b023934d7: Hey. We've recruited people into the resistance so far. We still need more.
textByHashes.1bf0fb44a8beb72611402afc7f07b35e56b902c9: We have the numbers, but the mourners are better trained and better equipped. You should make sure you're ready before we begin. There will be bloodshed, on both sides.
textByHashes.b34b051442b4cadf2974c3aee58e66a49e961d96: I don't like it, but I know it's the only way. I appreciate the concern though, thank you friend.
textByHashes.b247378d3ab09c559b06c9b9267f13578610a2fe: It is, it's just a shame there isn't another way.
textByHashes.60cbf9d4687734634c6530b4015e436823a724d2: No... But you're not the one I'm worried about.
textByHashes.8bfb9d5bcb1080cd02f6085b9731e83cc90c14b0: I will send word around to make sure our people are prepared. Good luck everyone.
textByHashes.571155518dd814c4fbe8eefa2710e8bf74dce524: Good work! Let's head downstairs and deal with the rest of them!
textByHashes.eccac22bd439e754c15221b7c96ef71a3dceba74: We did it! Now we'd better get out of here!
textByHashes.5ef98c6e56bf91bc0bdbca8176fe2c7618e6664c: We'll need to move fast. He's probably already planning his retaliation.
textByHashes.93d6cea594fe8883152096e8439e1e148253920a: We can afford to take a little bit of time to ensure everyone has resupplied so feel free to come back later if you're not ready yet. Just don't take too long.
textByHashes.a9169444ecfa8c4cb7c5788af596b6a1f5bce224: I never thought this day would come. We're finally going to deal with that traitor once and for all!
textByHashes.341af5e2b77e237e064de0217edf4dd06466c7c8: Look's like that's the last of them! Good work everyone!
textByHashes.158060ff2f27eb6e130a727707d248423cd77a89: Give it a rest, it's over. We know the truth.
textByHashes.c07468afe243f4c88fa659f6f436586b84ac75cf: is innocent in this. You manipulated them for your own gain.
textByHashes.952752ba8eae0a1da5e85746f000ee0c9ebdb4ae: So this is it. After all this time, Lathas is finally going to pay for what he's done. It's like I should feel something, but I just feel empty.
textByHashes.9da96963e538ce667c29b1e269d0dde5eff48ea6: is innocent! You lied to them!
textByHashes.6bd36b5e699953cf1dbfe14f43c79afbdfc309f1: I'm okay. So much has happened so fast, it's a lot to take in.
textByHashes.7e4bb00018db0c9aa2488a2cc785d130e5992779: I guess... I just don't know if I want to.
textByHashes.93cea6eb80cdfd52cc9a0774b300047d7bbcab7a: Anyway, I wanted to ask you something.
textByHashes.1237bc88ab216a72a732df712a59dab0eeffd513: The people of Ardougne, the elves here, you've spent a lot of time helping them. Why? What drives you, ?
textByHashes.6c643b427111e4700edcdd7d2aa29bf3a094841a: So even if you knew that you'd get nothing out of it yourself, you'd still continue to help these elves?
textByHashes.ea897af8655df7382b3acbcf5a2bdc510b7d9fc2: I suspect not many people would feel that way.
textByHashes.b04a9e3151947cf8f6e1c6ea142606beeed9874d: Clearly. Thank you for answering, I realise it's a pretty pointless question. I was curious to know how you felt though.
textByHashes.4b66921680e3f868f547c5568643ff31fe32e15f: I guess it's always nice to be rewarded. It's good to know that isn't all that matters to you though. I suspect not many people would feel that way.
textByHashes.721ccec32b7ed9c437c22bc9d09479393e3b37ca: I guess it's always nice to be rewarded. You should be careful not to let that drive you though. Money can't buy everything, .
textByHashes.5d24aa977f71898e1169d45bdfbaffaa7358fd0e: Well I've rambled on enough. Was there something you needed?
textByHashes.ef1c6c5bef59110b9202c21fedfa085444afb7a0: I'd better head over there then.
textByHashes.f32b1ecaac1eaa8802b508860c2ec54840af480e: So if you didn't think you'd get a reward, you'd stop helping these elves?
textByHashes.c3bb9e18b4678490b3b2fe0756347a33d6a6aaf8: You should be careful of letting that drive you. Money can't buy everything, .
textByHashes.7b23a329e75042b3e5f9c7f411399468d5c02308: So it's not just about the reward after all. It's nice to know that isn't all that matters to you. I suspect not many people would feel that way.
textByHashes.52843758776cce8fbb0105d3d608bf6fb95c9544: So it's about what happened with King Tyras? Do you think you'd still help these elves if that hadn't happened?
textByHashes.edde62a8bed33bbaed71aab82d6369d773f65cfc: So it's not just guilt that's driving you, that's good to know. What happened with King Tyras was awful but you can't take too much blame, Lathas manipulated you.
textByHashes.aef1cc8c9d8aec702f699853142990fa8eff4cff: So the only thing driving you is guilt? That doesn't seem healthy. Be careful that you don't carry the world on your shoulders, . It's not your fault that Lathas manipulated you.
textByHashes.c9c455107ca00a5a41f19b33129bdbc0f4c2e284: Hmmm, not something you've had to think about I guess. Just make sure that you don't carry the world on your shoulders, . It's not your fault that Lathas manipulated you.
textByHashes.c81dd0b5da05f3c858e9199fc257c91a0f10df35: Anyway, I've rambled on enough. Was there something you needed?
textByHashes.db113166057ec5c7673aec06cb0336b86e5a1885: I'd better head over there then. Thanks for the talk.
textByHashes.8819957ce8d03d94ee82f9f8fba6dc132481e6a8: Be careful of hiding behind duty, it won't stop people holding you accountable for your actions.
textByHashes.774d53da6734dfe3f314d17c9f707e34385258a9: Hmmm, not something you've had to think about I guess. It's worth considering though. We should all have something to drive us.
textByHashes.fa94873769e6d7423ed9f7743311c5198c872d35: Anyway, was there something you needed?
textByHashes.836d3fa284cfb7fdc5ca75d8578bcb9f3aaea0d8: Fair enough. Was there something you needed?
textByHashes.87176aeb016712691b651ce545a0c5f7c0cb8856: Hey . You should talk to Arianwyn when you're ready to begin.
textByHashes.c4b6030d2f41838f13f787b3cebee20a832fb385: And what about our people? While you sat here in your secure little village, we took action! We saved lives! But I guess you're too selfish to care about us.
textByHashes.6b7286127e84cb144f0457fad840f04147cef6c4: Sorry, but I don't understand what you mean by all of this. Who's Seren? What library?
textByHashes.70e36436c64ef3d8923327c30fe95797aaadaff5: I see, I'm sorry to hear that.
textByHashes.af15cde5c7f052bba87a05789e2be105c894c851: Is there no way we can check?
textByHashes.e3ebebc236d69372e58c210b40cceda57fa92a4c: Pretty good. I've been thinking about that elf, Ysgawyn.
textByHashes.d7bce6ad4960bbaaad05669078315c17be83f8fa: That's one way to put it. I can't say I approve of his attitude, but I appreciate he's seen a lot in this war.
textByHashes.2c6f7d42ca00ced1047d21a31dd39ac523f51d7e: It's interesting, the elves have such long lifespans, we're just children compared to them. And yet, even with all their experience, they're still just as fickle as we are.
textByHashes.21181cbbd041748994e1186cd49ef92bc999c935: Anyway, had any luck with Baxtorian Falls?
textByHashes.e41e7272adb17adb5dc729dfdffda68040631bdd: I'll let you crack on then. We'll talk more later.
textByHashes.e3db758dfa33f7d3058fddd935c8e6b05dc6b10b: A potion? Sounds like my area of expertise.
textByHashes.91824636d78f118bd120637cb72e9896b135984f: Right. , meet me by the pond. I could use your help with this.
textByHashes.bcedf8e92ef056141f4f4f6fe7b32b84eb42fa03: Hey . Glad everything went okay at Baxtorian Falls.
textByHashes.34ff91ee43e9eba720847c8d7d15c13b6c98f92d: Nope, all nice and quiet for a change.
textByHashes.fe78724ba13959c150492dab640a5d8f2b51eab4: Baxtorian is an interesting one, isn't he?
textByHashes.99cef219c1e568cf3b98b043b3106363c51cf9e9: He reminds me of a story my father once told me. A story of a broken man.
textByHashes.1a4081a87be3fe283f86da8a063766aa23a3a8cc: A long time ago, there were two brothers living in Avarrocka. They lived a simple life, farming the land and selling the produce to people in the city.
textByHashes.15b9bae1bf2d28a285c81ce8352b60901ad88b6b: When war came to the city, the king ordered that every able-bodied man take up arms against the enemy. The two brothers had to leave their home behind, to fight for a king they'd never met.
textByHashes.321f1105e9ce194db479f7e5339f12d144acf1f7: Like everyone else, they were given basic weapons and basic training. Enough to give them a chance, but not a big one. When the fighting started, they were poorly equipped and poorly prepared.
textByHashes.f8a44d39e52435ef19fe4693a2242e3389eac46f: They fought in many battles. Battles in strange places against strange people. As the war dragged on, they saw more and more horrors, from both sides. The only thing that kept them going was each other.
textByHashes.0597a49b9f31731ae6f69565bf03351c7da4c93b: As the war drew to a close, they were called to one final battle. The enemy knew they were losing, and they fought more ferociously than ever before.
textByHashes.21b958ce8a6da5bdc25541dcd7552d42d222605f: That was where it happened. One of the brothers was cut off and surrounded. He tried to fight back, but he didn't stand a chance.
textByHashes.23b5962da59d278c3ba46a74e36ee4fbf2321223: Eventually the fighting stopped, and the war was over. The other brother could finally go home. But the truth is, he had nothing to go home to.
textByHashes.fb68541f764cc6a65ff0dc45f85a1af9d771f455: It wasn't the farm that mattered, not really. The thing that mattered to him was gone.
textByHashes.bc77ccb74c1166ece57bca31bb801ea057a4e55e: In a way, he'd died back on the battlefield alongside his brother. Left in his place was just a broken man, with nothing left to live for.
textByHashes.ce8477b639352ce5a58fe2b935845e7d46e2873d: He lost his wife, his people and his kingdom. Now he's in a strange place, surrounded by strange people, helping to fight a war that he lost a long time ago.
textByHashes.681e0ea7c047fe7828ee9e19c18cb1232b682020: I guess you're right. The short of it is, Baxtorian has lost everything that he cared about. He's just an empty shell now, with nothing left to live for.
textByHashes.69850d93fed0bb1a72bb7112c4bfee5482624068: Maybe. I guess only time will tell.
textByHashes.c69926e8738452c24169544322150a745b9e2656: Right. Shall we get started with this potion?
textByHashes.7061869c16f957b0648aea1d6eef3dc8bf5a6fd7: Baxtorian gave me the details. To start with, we need an elder cadantine herb. We should be able to grow one from a normal cadantine seed, we just need to prepare the patch a little differently.
textByHashes.65438fbd33aa42bc3023f0424eebe3d350ca8986: The elves have said we can use this farming patch here. While I prepare the patch, could you go and fetch a cadantine seed and a seed dibber?
textByHashes.00ad807c976b0a9577271c3367cfcf484b73371c: Well it also happens that I've actually already prepared the patch, so you can get started right away.
textByHashes.2186313c3f32263e5e3e2450fa27bb61acedf292: Indeed. When you're ready, go ahead and plant the seed.
textByHashes.bed9913f0f3390b6c7a87c31a0d404f5499eb67d: Hey . Do you have that seed yet?
textByHashes.ee7efc0020f21251e9067f45d979cb93573d3a73: It's a cadantine seed that we need. Let me know once you have one. You'll want to get a seed dibber as well so we can plant it.
textByHashes.829b82e7959e9b3320f366371113c18bdd90aaf5: Great. The patch is all prepared so just plant it when you're ready.
textByHashes.be3135e686857daa3ed6fc5e1d0698f20afd2be8: Great. I'll keep an eye on the patch while it grows. You should check in with Arianwyn and Baxtorian in the meantime.
textByHashes.6876d7a8bc2e78006ef201449aa72da8138c6fbf: Hey . The elder cadantine herb grew perfectly. Let's get it harvested.
textByHashes.edb533665accafd01cf20ae182ebbb2bee7d5795: The other ingredient is crystal dust. Hopefully you can get some from Arianwyn or Baxtorian. Once you've got some dust, just add it to a vial of water along with the herb.
textByHashes.1352f002fb7afbc29a739f356d24256fc59409c0: Add the elder cadantine to a vial of water and then add some crystal dust. Hopefully either Arianwyn or Baxtorian can give you some crystal dust.
textByHashes.0378baaca40d2989c4be82d4b0e5db4613378a11: Great, you should take it to Arianwyn and Baxtorian.
textByHashes.b28894548b858ed077037be6110161c921f3e4f0: Hey Good luck in the library. We'll talk more when you get back.
textByHashes.9bca0672ea1ea1015baacbdca21b3e714555de54: Hey . You'd better speak with Arianwyn if you're done in the library.
textByHashes.57cd3d9f21d35a52d8bdbba9579f63ae239c33c5: I saw him heading out into the forest earlier. He must have wanted some time alone.
textByHashes.9e48649492791bb60aeff1baf73d47eeafd77658: Not too bad. I've been given a lot to think about though. Everything I've learnt about Seren, about the elves... it's crazy how big it all is. Our little fight in Ardougne seems so small now.
textByHashes.3d928969418e011bf6a4eb5c38d66d841f2f21f1: Now you know me better than that, I'm too stubborn to leave now.
textByHashes.c837d24c0d396a9793274e57b6e073a1fd8fea74: It's about closure I guess. So many people were hurt by Lord Iorwerth's actions in West Ardougne. I need to know that he'll never hurt them again.
textByHashes.0116ef16f7671d18abf789b5f9d2ff1631b688b2: Maybe his cause is just, but that doesn't give him the right to ruin lives in the way he has.
textByHashes.7b3854f391f1d8c0fec6b02dabd2d93d0d8fa55c: You mean do I think he should die?
textByHashes.e0f9ee462b0655ef7e68480773381d886068dfcb: I've tried to dedicate my life to helping people, healing them. I think all lives are valuable, even the lives of people like Lord Iorwerth.
textByHashes.071125e95da6dfe4d87899a5084a7665f4e5c594: I think Lord Iorwerth should face justice for what he has done. I don't think killing him is justice though. Maybe that makes me weak, but that's how I feel.
textByHashes.cbc60b703cfbdc6c35dbed4d5577546202fd52f8: You do? Thank you, that means a lot, especially from you.
textByHashes.3975ec55cc028c9ada519ca908223a60290b4a83: I realise I must come across as so self-righteous with all of this. I'm sorry about that.
textByHashes.5d98a54e7a67fbcd58c08cb832516cf5d39c7bbb: As much as I hate it, I know that sometimes death is the only option. I like to think we can consider alternatives though. It's what sets us apart from the enemy.
textByHashes.9f49191da6190dca1db027da0f92277328966620: He does, but does it need to be with his life? Killing is a solution, but maybe it's not the only one.
textByHashes.dc8f250547a06a12e5d47393027aae59ba55e447: I'm sorry. I realise I must come across as so self- righteous talking like this. I'm not trying to act better than anyone, I just want to make sure we've considered every option.
textByHashes.74024b5e159cc1e0daee636b6a319d6eef55aa99: I suppose... I just can't help but wonder if there might be another way.
textByHashes.2bafa14bc73f958b8b8d94d32a304d69368b8b86: I did. I don't know where he went though.
textByHashes.25e86516847f409c6b9d5b3c38076ce257b8e2b4: He only left recently, maybe you can pick up his trail. Have a look around the hidden entrance to the village, by the wolves, and see if you can find anything out of place.
textByHashes.a576ea57552687503506f3d4a2b2805c291fe74f: Hey . Thanks for the talk earlier, it meant a lot. I won't keep you, I'm sure you've got things to do. See you later
textByHashes.cfebc99005c1a1eab4709d3b0958d397feb63052: Hi there. I'm sure you've got things to do. You should probably go do them.
textByHashes.220aa72beaba46f6744091d31d4c89e3578e0651: I'm fine. We need to get the citizens to safety!
textByHashes.ba9d521d0b26ef4dd5146428b78e9da37e3e688e: , you go help Mawrth. We'll get the other citizens to safety.
textByHashes.00277e56d7c5fc5a04e2554545e2c48e9c88ecd9: Go! I'll get Mawrth and the other citizens to safety!
textByHashes.9c3025eb13ceed9f7a7391cbebc0f19dbde9ff88: Making sure I've got everything ready. I'll be leaving for the Underground Pass soon.
textByHashes.478702ca045ca08350cf96227500ec599af722be: Well I've stuck around for this long, it would feel wrong to leave now. Besides, I may not be much of a fighter, but I can help in other ways.
textByHashes.b9deb1fae2cfa87aa732ea7a80e5c26f391a59c8: Hey. I think Baxtorian wants to talk to you.
textByHashes.5c4f47a00c4463a81ae71524c31ccf81050b8aec: So this is it, after everything that's happened, this is where it ends.
textByHashes.04be7bbb7a7ea3468b75e7d588f25156aa86adad: You... You matter to me you know...
textByHashes.26436931cff71a527c4c9458f0027c7717456072: Thank you , you're a good friend.
textByHashes.e55a2f8cb81b9864509af3799add837561e2e392: I... I'm terrible at these moments. I never know what to say.
textByHashes.d0dac977fec0d7022ee81d92bfd63945a1754921: That's right. One of the dwarves here, Kamen, has brewed up some strange alcohol. Whatever it is, it's very flammable.
textByHashes.1783cbcbf0bb7b7bb5f8501b234d2f135eaa548c: Well I think we can make use of it again. I've combined the alcohol with some other chemicals. The result is quite explosive!
textByHashes.6e75b254950bfde1c72e15e514325b77ae6eb220: If we position some of these explosives on the approach to the camp, we can use them against the Iorwerth forces.
textByHashes.2a5083b354a311790d2f39042dbe0d70be474081: I've dug some holes around the camp for the explosives to go in. Could you fill them in for me?
textByHashes.aac786b8d8786b9fb8238e2970e5b266f5e1df37: Here's the explosives. If you need more just let me know.
textByHashes.93d78619d8e6f9aff6473bf55fca3cc5e1a5ea45: They've also agreed to let me move into the city. That tiny house in Ardougne was becoming a bit tiresome, and there's so much for me to learn from the Meilyr elves.
textByHashes.6274c96d8cb97108cbfd47c950e5946f274bc720: Not bad. I won't lie though, the stuff you told me about elves and dark lords is all a bit frightening.
textByHashes.f6c16aff159596a905bbdf78a8a330ae35e94cb6: I hope you're right. Be careful out there, I worry about you.
textByHashes.1f28c54214d154ae41bd7a1bc1336bbb205fda28: Well it just so happens that by freak accident, I had hundreds delivered the other day!
